title: A study on the advancement of building energy simulations: Utilizing ChatGPT to enhance occupancy rate predictions and sustainability outcomes
inputData: 文本（职业类型+时间表描述）
contribution: 提出利用ChatGPT基于职业类型和用户特征预测建筑 occupancy rate 的方法，通过120次重复问答提取逐时使用率数据，经验证与问卷调查结果一致性优于ASHRAE 90.1和DesignBuilder内置参考值
markdownFile: Lee et al. - 2025 - A study on the advancement of building energy simulations Utilizing ChatGPT to enhance occupancy ra.md
pdfFile: Lee et al. - 2025 - A study on the advancement of building energy simulations Utilizing ChatGPT to enhance occupancy ra.pdf
abstract: This study proposes a methodology for estimating building occupancy rates based on building usage and user characteristics, utilizing ChatGPT. The reliability of the occupancy schedules generated by ChatGPT was confirmed through a survey of 318 respondents, and the methodology exhibited higher consistency compared to existing published occupancy rate references (ASHRAE 90.1 and DesignBuilder occupancy library). Energy simulations using DesignBuilder on a real residential apartment in Busan, Korea, showed that simulation results with ChatGPT-generated occupancy rates more closely matched actual measured energy consumption patterns than those using default occupancy libraries.
journal: International Journal of Air-Conditioning and Refrigeration
methodology: The study uses ChatGPT (GPT-4) to generate occupancy schedules by repeatedly asking 120 questions per occupational group (college students, office workers, housewives, unemployed persons, freelancers) about daily routines on an hourly basis (0:00-24:00), covering weekday and weekend patterns, locations within housing units, and transportation. The probabilistic responses are converted into occupancy rates by space and time. A survey of 318 respondents was conducted via Google Forms and SNS to validate the ChatGPT-generated occupancy schedules. Energy simulations were performed in DesignBuilder for a real 33rd-floor apartment unit in Busan, Korea, comparing three cases: Case 1 (DesignBuilder default occupancy library), Case 2 (ChatGPT-generated occupancy rates), and Case 3 (actual measured energy consumption over 31 days in August).
results: ChatGPT-generated occupancy rates showed lower average errors (weekday 0.106, weekend 0.130) against survey results than DesignBuilder library (weekday 0.244, weekend 0.244) and were competitive with ASHRAE 90.1 (weekday 0.148, weekend 0.147). In energy simulation, Case 2 (ChatGPT) total energy consumption was 262.26 kWh electricity and 138.8 kWh gas, compared to Case 1 (DesignBuilder) at 329.48 kWh and 178.56 kWh, and Case 3 (measured) at 249 kWh and 66.51 kWh. ChatGPT-based simulations better captured hourly energy usage patterns, particularly weekday afternoon vacancy and weekend hot water consumption peaks, demonstrating closer alignment with actual measured data.
